node.title: Uber | VO | Find longest path in graph

topic.title: Uber | VO | Find longest path in graph

post.content:
Given a DAG, return the longest path in the graph.

Input: String[][]
[["NYC","SFO"], ["SFO","LAX"],["LAX","SEA"],["PDX","SEA"]]

Return: List<String>
NYC -> SFO -> LAX -> SEA

- Build a map (adjacencies) with the adjacency list (key = origin, value = destination), value is probably a list since from one node you are likely to be able to more than one node\n- Build a second map (lengths) where (key = origin, value = longest path)\n- DFS from every origin to each of their destinations, if the origin has already a value in the lengths map, skip it to not do repeated work.
Is it not just the levels in the topological sort?
